target.name: Modern Intelligence
target.founder: John Dulin, Joe Cieslik, Tristan Tager

methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $5M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Defense & Security (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 19 same-sector peers exist in Defense & Security, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use

data.narrative:
Modern Intelligence is a seed-stage defense and security company founded by John Dulin, Joe Cieslik, and Tristan Tager that has raised $5.12M to date. The valuation universe for Defense & Security includes 2 public comparables (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) and 8 private peers including Anduril Industries, Helsing, Shield AI, Saronic, and Quantum-Systems.

Stage-progression methodology is the primary applicable valuation approach. Using the company's $5.12M total raised as a base proxy and applying a typical 2.5x multiplier for progression from seed to next stage yields an implied valuation of $13M. This method reflects the capital deployed to date and the expected step-up in company value upon reaching Series A. Peer-median analysis was not viable due to insufficient comparable data points; while 19 same-sector peers exist, none met the filters for same-stage, adjacent-stage, or similar-tier comparability (minimum 3 required), making broader sector medians unreliable for this early-stage, specialized subsector.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $11M (low) to $15M (high), with a median point of $13M. This single-method triangulation carries meaningful uncertainty: seed-stage defense tech companies exhibit wide variance in stage progression multiples, and the lack of tight peer comparables limits benchmarking confidence. Key risks to valuation include customer concentration, regulatory dependencies, and the founder team's execution track record in commercializing frontier defense applications.
